properties.description: Central park in downtown Shanghai, popular for morning tai chi and the famous 'marriage market' on weekends
properties.sustainability_notes: Converted from the pre-1949 Shanghai Race Club grounds into a public park in 1952; provides approximately 10 ha of green space in the highest-density part of the urban core, serving as a heat-island mitigation buffer and the primary accessible green space for residents of central Huangpu District.
properties.notes: Adjacent to Shanghai Museum. Good rest stop between Nanjing Road and The Bund.

properties.description: Former French Concession park laid out in formal French garden style (1909), beloved for its rose garden, shaded avenues, and lively morning exercise scenes with locals dancing and playing music
properties.sustainability_notes: One of Shanghai's oldest surviving designed parks (1909); its mature plane-tree canopy provides measurable urban cooling — Shanghai Municipal Greening Bureau data indicates street-tree canopy reduces ambient temperature by 2-4°C in surrounding French Concession blocks. The park functions as the social commons of the district, supporting daily exercise and community gathering without commercial infrastructure.
properties.notes: Come in the morning to see locals doing ballroom dancing, tai chi, and badminton. The surrounding French Concession streets are great for a post-park stroll. Rose garden is at its best in May.

properties.description: Shanghai's largest inner-city park at 140 hectares, with a large lake, cycling paths, and landscaped gardens in the heart of Pudong — a green escape from the nearby skyscrapers
properties.sustainability_notes: At 140 ha, Century Park is the largest green lung in Pudong and one of the few spaces in the district providing meaningful stormwater retention — the central lake (approximately 20 ha) functions as an urban wetland buffer. Shanghai's 2035 Master Plan designates the park as a key biodiversity habitat corridor linking the Huangpu riverfront with the eastern wetlands.
properties.notes: Bicycles and electric scooters available to rent inside the park. Picnic friendly. Convenient when combined with a visit to the Shanghai Science & Technology Museum (adjacent at Century Ave metro stop).

properties.description: The landscaped riverside promenade and green space running along The Bund waterfront, offering unobstructed views of Pudong and a pleasant walking and cycling path along the Huangpu River
properties.address: Zhongshan East 1st Rd riverside, Huangpu District
properties.sustainability_notes: The 2010 Bund waterfront renewal project converted a six-lane elevated highway into a pedestrian riverside park, reclaiming approximately 2.5 km of riverbank for non-motorised public use and reducing vehicle emissions in the heritage zone.
properties.notes: The riverfront park extends south from The Bund past the Old Town all the way to the South Bund. Walk or rent a shared bike to cover the full stretch. The best sunrise spot in central Shanghai.

properties.description: 186-hectare urban forest in northeastern Shanghai with dense woodland, wetland areas, a lake, and rare bird populations — a genuine forest experience within city limits
properties.sustainability_notes: Designated urban biodiversity reserve under Shanghai's ecological network plan; over 160 bird species recorded including nationally protected raptors, making it a key stopover on the East Asian–Australasian Flyway. The 186 ha of contiguous woodland provide carbon sequestration estimated at approximately 3,500 tonnes CO2/year (Shanghai Municipal Forestry Bureau data).
properties.notes: Less visited by tourists, so more authentic than central parks. Birdwatching is excellent especially during spring migration (March-May). Horse riding and boat rental available. Reachable by Metro Line 8.
